{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c3c2c39c72ae45928d5e7c8c","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→```xml\n     2→<project_specification>\n     3→  <project_name>Image Detection Result Analyzer - Frontend Tool</project_name>\n     4→\n     5→  <overview>\n     6→    Build a comprehensive frontend tool with a Flask backend to analyze the performance of object detection models by comparing ground truth annotations with model predictions. The application will process local datasets consisting of image sequences, ground truth bounding box JSON files, and model prediction JSON files (containing bounding boxes, classes, and confidence scores). It provides two core views: a statistics dashboard displaying per-class detection metrics (recall, false positive rate, etc.) and an image-by-image comparison view showing visual overlays of ground truth and predicted bounding boxes with distinct visual indicators for correct detections, false positives, and missed detections. The tool prioritizes intuitive visualization, accurate metric calculation, and seamless local file handling.\n     7→  </overview>\n     8→\n     9→  <technology_stack>\n    10→    <frontend>\n    11→      <framework>Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)</framework>\n    12→      <styling>Custom CSS with Flexbox/Grid, optional Bootstrap 5 (via CDN) for responsive layout</styling>\n    13→      <visualization>Chart.js (CDN) for statistical charts, Canvas API for bounding box rendering</visualization>\n    14→      <file_handling>FileReader API for local file processing</file_handling>\n    15→      <routing>Client-side routing with JavaScript (hash-based) for page navigation</routing>\n    16→      <icons>Font Awesome (CDN) for visual indicators (check, cross, dash)</icons>\n    17→    </frontend>\n    18→    <backend>\n    19→      <runtime>Python 3.8+</runtime>\n    20→      <framework>Flask 2.0+</framework>\n    21→      <data_processing>\n    22→        - JSON parsing with built-in json module\n    23→        - Image processing with Pillow (PIL) for thumbnail generation\n    24→        - Metric calculation (recall, precision, FPR) with numpy\n    25→      </data_processing>\n    26→      <cors>Flask-CORS for cross-origin resource sharing</cors>\n    27→      <file_management>Built-in os/pathlib for local file system interaction</file_management>\n    28→    </backend>\n    29→    <communication>\n    30→      <api>RESTful endpoints for data processing and retrieval</api>\n    31→      <data_format>JSON for all data exchange between frontend and backend</data_format>\n    32→      <file_upload>Multipart/form-data for batch file upload (optional) or local file path specification</file_handling>\n    33→    </communication>\n    34→    <dependencies>\n    35→      <python>\n    36→        - flask>=2.0.0\n    37→        - flask-cors>=3.0.10\n    38→        - pillow>=9.0.0\n    39→        - numpy>=1.21.0\n    40→      </python>\n    41→      <frontend_cdn>\n    42→        - Chart.js v4.4.0\n    43→        - Bootstrap 5.3.0 (optional)\n    44→        - Font Awesome 6.4.0\n    45→      </frontend_cdn>\n    46→    </dependencies>\n    47→  </technology_stack>\n    48→\n    49→  <prerequisites>\n    50→    <environment_setup>\n    51→      - Python 3.8+ installed on the system\n    52→      - Flask and required dependencies installed via pip: pip install flask flask-cors pillow numpy\n    53→      - Local dataset organized in a structured directory (images in /images, ground truth JSON in /gt, predictions JSON in /predictions)\n    54→      - Backend code in /app directory with app.py as entry point\n    55→      - Frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS) in /app/static directory\n    56→      - Template files in /app/templates directory\n    57→      - Environment variables (optional) for dataset path configuration (.env file with DATASET_PATH)\n    58→    </environment_setup>\n    59→    <dataset_format>\n    60→      <image_sequence>\n    61→        - All images in a single directory (JPG/PNG format) with consistent naming convention (e.g., img_001.jpg, img_002.png)\n    62→        - Image dimensions consistent or variable (tool should handle both)\n    63→      </image_sequence>\n    64→      <ground_truth_json>\n    65→        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001.json)\n    66→        - Structure: {\n    67→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n    68→            \"width\": 1920,\n    69→            \"height\": 1080,\n    70→            \"annotations\": [\n    71→              {\n    72→                \"class\": \"car\",\n    73→                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2]  # pixel coordinates (top-left, bottom-right)\n    74→              },\n    75→              ...\n    76→            ]\n    77→          }\n    78→      </ground_truth_json>\n    79→      <prediction_json>\n    80→        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001_pred.json)\n    81→        - Structure: {\n    82→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n    83→            \"width\": 1920,\n    84→            \"height\": 1080,\n    85→            \"predictions\": [\n    86→              {\n    87→                \"class\": \"car\",\n    88→                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2],\n    89→                \"score\": 0.95  # confidence score (0-1)\n    90→              },\n    91→              ...\n    92→            ]\n    93→          }\n    94→      </prediction_json>\n    95→    </dataset_format>\n    96→  </prerequisites>\n    97→\n    98→  <core_features>\n    99→    <file_handling>\n   100→      - Local dataset path configuration (input field for directory path)\n   101→      - Validation of dataset structure (check for images, GT JSON, prediction JSON)\n   102→      - Batch parsing of all files in the dataset directory\n   103→      - Error handling for missing files, invalid JSON formats, and mismatched filenames\n   104→      - Thumbnail generation for all images (150x150px, aspect ratio preserved)\n   105→      - Caching of parsed data to avoid reprocessing on page refresh\n   106→    </file_handling>\n   107→\n   108→    <statistics_dashboard>\n   109→      <metrics_calculation>\n   110→        - **Recall (True Positive Rate)**: TP / (TP + FN) per class\n   111→        - **Precision**: TP / (TP + FP) per class\n   112→        - **False Positive Rate (FPR)**: FP / (FP + TN) per class\n   113→        - **F1 Score**: 2 * (Precision * Recall) / (Precision + Recall) per class\n   114→        - **Overall Accuracy**: (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN)\n   115→        - **Average Precision (AP)** per class (optional)\n   116→        - **Intersection over Union (IoU)** threshold configuration (default: 0.5) for TP/FP/FN classification\n   117→      </metrics_calculation>\n   118→      <visualization>\n   119→        - Bar charts for per-class recall, precision, FPR\n   120→        - Pie charts for class distribution in ground truth vs predictions\n   121→        - Summary table with all metrics for easy comparison\n   122→        - Sortable and filterable metric table (by class name, metric value)\n   123→        - Export statistics to CSV/JSON format\n   124→        - Interactive tooltips on charts showing exact metric values\n   125→      </visualization>\n   126→      <user_interface>\n   127→        - Dropdown for IoU threshold adjustment (0.3 to 0.9 in 0.1 increments)\n   128→        - Class filter (show/hide specific classes in charts/tables)\n   129→        - Confidence score threshold filter for predictions (0.0 to 1.0 slider)\n   130→        - Reset filters button to restore default settings\n   131→        - Summary card with key overall metrics (total images, total classes, average recall/precision)\n   132→      </user_interface>\n   133→    </statistics_dashboard>\n   134→\n   135→    <image_comparison_view>\n   136→      <left_panel>\n   137→        - Searchable and filterable file list (by filename, class presence)\n   138→        - Image thumbnails alongside filenames (150x150px)\n   139→        - Scrollable list with fixed height (occupies 100% of viewport height)\n   140→        - Highlight active/selected image in the list\n   141→        - Pagination (optional) for large datasets (10/20/50 images per page)\n   142→        - Class filter (show only images containing specific classes)\n   143→        - Status filter (show only images with FP/FN/perfect detections)\n   144→      </left_panel>\n   145→      <right_panel>\n   146→        <image_display>\n   147→          - High-resolution image view (scaled to fit viewport, maintain aspect ratio)\n   148→          - Interactive canvas overlay for bounding boxes\n   149→          - Bounding box styles:\n   150→            - True Positive (TP): Green border (2px), green checkmark (✓) at top-left corner\n   151→            - False Positive (FP): Red border (2px), red cross (✗) at top-left corner\n   152→            - False Negative (FN): Red border (2px), red horizontal dash (—) at top-left corner\n   153→          - Bounding box labels (class name + confidence score for predictions)\n   154→          - Zoom/pan functionality for detailed inspection\n   155→          - Toggle visibility of ground truth/prediction boxes\n   156→          - Toggle visibility of box labels/indicators\n   157→        </image_display>\n   158→        <per_image_statistics>\n   159→          - Summary card for current image (filename, dimensions, total GT boxes, total predicted boxes)\n   160→          - Per-class breakdown table (GT count, predicted count, TP, FP, FN for each class)\n   161→          - IoU values for each matched bounding box (optional tooltip on hover)\n   162→          - Confidence score distribution for predictions on current image\n   163→          - Navigation buttons (previous/next image) for sequential viewing\n   164→        </per_image_statistics>\n   165→      </right_panel>\n   166→    </image_comparison_view>\n   167→\n   168→    <navigation>\n   169→      - id (primary key, integer)\n   199→        - path (text, unique)\n   200→        - total_images (integer)\n   201→        - total_classes (integer)\n   202→        - iou_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n   203→        - confidence_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n   204→        - created_at (timestamp)\n   205→        - last_updated (timestamp)\n   206→      </dataset_metadata>\n   207→\n   208→      <image_metadata>\n   209→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   210→        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n   211→        - filename (text)\n   212→        - width (integer)\n   213→        - height (integer)\n   214→        - thumbnail_path (text)\n   215→        - total_gt_boxes (integer)\n   216→        - total_pred_boxes (integer)\n   217→        - has_fp (boolean)\n   218→        - has_fn (boolean)\n   219→        - is_perfect (boolean)\n   220→      </image_metadata>\n   221→\n   222→      <classes>\n   223→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   224→        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n   225→        - name (text, unique per dataset)\n   226→        - total_gt_count (integer)\n   227→        - total_pred_count (integer)\n   228→        - tp_count (integer)\n   229→        - fp_count (integer)\n   230→        - fn_count (integer)\n   231→        - recall (float)\n   232→        - precision (float)\n   233→        - fpr (float)\n   234→        - f1_score (float)\n   235→      </classes>\n   236→\n   237→      <bounding_boxes>\n   238→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   239→        - image_id (foreign key to image_metadata.id)\n   240→        - class_id (foreign key to classes.id)\n   241→        - type (text, enum: 'ground_truth', 'prediction')\n   242→        - x1 (float)\n   243→        - y1 (float)\n   244→        - x2 (float)\n   245→        - y2 (float)\n   246→        - confidence (float, null for ground truth)\n   247→        - iou (float, null for unmatched boxes)\n   248→        - classification (text, enum: 'tp', 'fp', 'fn', null for unclassified)\n   249→      </bounding_boxes>\n   250→    </tables>\n   251→    <notes>\n   252→      - Database is SQLite (file-based) for simplicity, stored as dataset_analysis.db\n   253→      - Data is cleared/overwritten when a new dataset is loaded\n   254→      - Indexes on filename, class name, and image_id for faster queries\n   255→    </notes>\n   256→  </database_schema>\n   257→\n   258→  <api_endpoints_summary>\n   259→    <dataset>\n   260→      - POST /api/dataset/load: Load and parse dataset from provided path\n   261→        - Request body: { \"dataset_path\": \"/path/to/dataset\", \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n   262→        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"dataset_id\": 1, \"total_images\": 100, \"total_classes\": 5, \"errors\": [] }\n   263→      - GET /api/dataset/metadata/:id: Get dataset metadata and summary statistics\n   264→      - DELETE /api/dataset/:id: Clear dataset from memory/database\n   265→    </dataset>\n   266→\n   267→    <statistics>\n   268→      - GET /api/statistics/:dataset_id: Get all per-class statistics\n   269→        - Response: { \"classes\": [...], \"overall_metrics\": {...}, \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n   270→      - POST /api/statistics/recalculate: Recalculate metrics with new thresholds\n   271→        - Request body: { \"dataset_id\": 1, \"iou_threshold\": 0.6, \"confidence_threshold\": 0.7 }\n   272→        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"statistics\": {...} }\n   273→      - GET /api/statistics/export/:dataset_id: Export statistics to CSV/JSON\n   274→        - Query params: { \"format\": \"csv\" }\n   275→    </statistics>\n   276→\n   277→    <images>\n   278→      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id: Get list of all images with metadata (filename, thumbnail path, basic stats)\n   279→        - Response: { \n   282→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\", \n   283→            \"dimensions\": { \"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080 },\n   284→            \"ground_truth_boxes\": [...],\n   285→            \"prediction_boxes\": [...],\n   286→            \"per_class_stats\": {...},\n   287→            \"thumbnail_path\": \"/static/thumbnails/img_001.jpg\"\n   288→          }\n   289→      - GET /api/images/thumbnail/:dataset_id/:filename: Serve generated thumbnail image\n   290→    </images>\n   291→\n   292→    <classes>\n   293→      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id: Get list of all classes in dataset with counts\n   294→      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id/:class_id: Get detailed statistics for single class\n   295→    </classes>\n   296→\n   297→    <health>\n   298→      - GET /api/health: Check server status and version\n   299→        - The application will process local datasets consisting of image sequences, ground truth bounding box JSON files, and model prediction JSON files (containing bounding boxes, classes, and confidence scores). It provides two core views: a statistics dashboard displaying per-class detection metrics (recall, false positive rate, etc.) and an image-by-image comparison view showing visual overlays of ground truth and predicted bounding boxes with distinct visual indicators for correct detections, false positives, and missed detections. The tool prioritizes intuitive visualization, accurate metric calculation, and seamless local file handling.\n  </overview>\n\n  <technology_stack>\n    <frontend>\n      <framework>Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)</framework>\n      <styling>Custom CSS with Flexbox/Grid, optional Bootstrap 5 (via CDN) for responsive layout</styling>\n      <visualization>Chart.js (CDN) for statistical charts, Canvas API for bounding box rendering</visualization>\n      <file_handling>FileReader API for local file processing</file_handling>\n      <routing>Client-side routing with JavaScript (hash-based) for page navigation</routing>\n      <icons>Font Awesome (CDN) for visual indicators (check, cross, dash)</icons>\n    </frontend>\n    <backend>\n      <runtime>Python 3.8+</runtime>\n      <framework>Flask 2.0+</framework>\n      <data_processing>\n        - JSON parsing with built-in json module\n        - Image processing with Pillow (PIL) for thumbnail generation\n        - Metric calculation (recall, precision, FPR) with numpy\n      </data_processing>\n      <cors>Flask-CORS for cross-origin resource sharing</cors>\n      <file_management>Built-in os/pathlib for local file system interaction</file_management>\n    </backend>\n    <communication>\n      <api>RESTful endpoints for data processing and retrieval</api>\n      <data_format>JSON for all data exchange between frontend and backend</data_format>\n      <file_upload>Multipart/form-data for batch file upload (optional) or local file path specification</file_handling>\n    </communication>\n    <dependencies>\n      <python>\n        - flask>=2.0.0\n        - flask-cors>=3.0.10\n        - pillow>=9.0.0\n        - numpy>=1.21.0\n      </python>\n      <frontend_cdn>\n        - Chart.js v4.4.0\n        - Bootstrap 5.3.0 (optional)\n        - Font Awesome 6.4.0\n      </frontend_cdn>\n    </dependencies>\n  </technology_stack>\n\n  <prerequisites>\n    <environment_setup>\n      - Python 3.8+ installed on the system\n      - Flask and required dependencies installed via pip: pip install flask flask-cors pillow numpy\n      - Local dataset organized in a structured directory (images in /images, ground truth JSON in /gt, predictions JSON in /predictions)\n      - Backend code in /app directory with app.py as entry point\n      - Frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS) in /app/static directory\n      - Template files in /app/templates directory\n      - Environment variables (optional) for dataset path configuration (.env file with DATASET_PATH)\n    </environment_setup>\n    <dataset_format>\n      <image_sequence>\n        - All images in a single directory (JPG/PNG format) with consistent naming convention (e.g., img_001.jpg, img_002.png)\n        - Image dimensions consistent or variable (tool should handle both)\n      </image_sequence>\n      <ground_truth_json>\n        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001.json)\n        - Structure: {\n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n            \"width\": 1920,\n            \"height\": 1080,\n            \"annotations\": [\n              {\n                \"class\": \"car\",\n                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2]  # pixel coordinates (top-left, bottom-right)\n              },\n              ...\n            ]\n          }\n      </ground_truth_json>\n      <prediction_json>\n        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001_pred.json)\n        - Structure: {\n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n            \"width\": 1920,\n            \"height\": 1080,\n            \"predictions\": [\n              {\n                \"class\": \"car\",\n                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2],\n                \"score\": 0.95  # confidence score (0-1)\n              },\n              ...\n            ]\n          }\n      </prediction_json>\n    </dataset_format>\n  </prerequisites>\n\n  <core_features>\n    <file_handling>\n      - Local dataset path configuration (input field for directory path)\n      - Validation of dataset structure (check for images, GT JSON, prediction JSON)\n      - Batch parsing of all files in the dataset directory\n      - Error handling for missing files, invalid JSON formats, and mismatched filenames\n      - Thumbnail generation for all images (150x150px, aspect ratio preserved)\n      - Caching of parsed data to avoid reprocessing on page refresh\n    </file_handling>\n\n    <statistics_dashboard>\n      <metrics_calculation>\n        - **Recall (True Positive Rate)**: TP / (TP + FN) per class\n        - **Precision**: TP / (TP + FP) per class\n        - **False Positive Rate (FPR)**: FP / (FP + TN) per class\n        - **F1 Score**: 2 * (Precision * Recall) / (Precision + Recall) per class\n        - **Overall Accuracy**: (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN)\n        - **Average Precision (AP)** per class (optional)\n        - **Intersection over Union (IoU)** threshold configuration (default: 0.5) for TP/FP/FN classification\n      </metrics_calculation>\n      <visualization>\n        - Bar charts for per-class recall, precision, FPR\n        - Pie charts for class distribution in ground truth vs predictions\n        - Summary table with all metrics for easy comparison\n        - Sortable and filterable metric table (by class name, metric value)\n        - Export statistics to CSV/JSON format\n        - Interactive tooltips on charts showing exact metric values\n      </visualization>\n      <user_interface>\n        - Dropdown for IoU threshold adjustment (0.3 to 0.9 in 0.1 increments)\n        - Class filter (show/hide specific classes in charts/tables)\n        - Confidence score threshold filter for predictions (0.0 to 1.0 slider)\n        - Reset filters button to restore default settings\n        - Summary card with key overall metrics (total images, total classes, average recall/precision)\n      </user_interface>\n    </statistics_dashboard>\n\n    <image_comparison_view>\n      <left_panel>\n        - Searchable and filterable file list (by filename, class presence)\n        - Image thumbnails alongside filenames (150x150px)\n        - Scrollable list with fixed height (occupies 100% of viewport height)\n        - Highlight active/selected image in the list\n        - Pagination (optional) for large datasets (10/20/50 images per page)\n        - Class filter (show only images containing specific classes)\n        - Status filter (show only images with FP/FN/perfect detections)\n      </left_panel>\n      <right_panel>\n        <image_display>\n          - High-resolution image view (scaled to fit viewport, maintain aspect ratio)\n          - Interactive canvas overlay for bounding boxes\n          - Bounding box styles:\n            - True Positive (TP): Green border (2px), green checkmark (✓) at top-left corner\n            - False Positive (FP): Red border (2px), red cross (✗) at top-left corner\n            - False Negative (FN): Red border (2px), red horizontal dash (—) at top-left corner\n          - Bounding box labels (class name + confidence score for predictions)\n          - Zoom/pan functionality for detailed inspection\n          - Toggle visibility of ground truth/prediction boxes\n          - Toggle visibility of box labels/indicators\n        </image_display>\n        <per_image_statistics>\n          - Summary card for current image (filename, dimensions, total GT boxes, total predicted boxes)\n          - Per-class breakdown table (GT count, predicted count, TP, FP, FN for each class)\n          - IoU values for each matched bounding box (optional tooltip on hover)\n          - Confidence score distribution for predictions on current image\n          - Navigation buttons (previous/next image) for sequential viewing\n        </per_image_statistics>\n      </right_panel>\n    </image_comparison_view>\n\n    <navigation>\n      - Top navigation bar with links to Statistics Dashboard and Image Comparison View\n      - Active page indicator in navigation bar\n      - Breadcrumb navigation for context (current dataset path, current image)\n      - Back to top button for long pages (statistics dashboard)\n      - Responsive navigation (collapsible menu on mobile devices)\n    </navigation>\n\n    <error_handling>\n      - User-friendly error messages for invalid dataset paths\n      - Validation errors for malformed JSON files (line number + error description)\n      - Warning messages for mismatched filenames (GT/prediction JSON without corresponding image)\n      - Loading spinners during data processing/file parsing\n      - Empty state handling (no data loaded, no images to display)\n      - Fallback UI for unsupported image formats\n    </error_handling>\n\n    <accessibility>\n      - Keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter for list selection, arrow keys for image navigation)\n      - Alt text for all images and icons\n      - High contrast mode for bounding boxes (optional toggle)\n      - Screen reader compatible labels for all UI elements\n      - Responsive design for mobile/tablet/desktop viewing\n      - Text size adjustment option (small/medium/large)\n    </accessibility>\n  </core_features>\n\n  <database_schema>\n    <tables>\n      <dataset_metadata>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - path (text, unique)\n        - total_images (integer)\n        - total_classes (integer)\n        - iou_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n        - confidence_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n        - created_at (timestamp)\n        - last_updated (timestamp)\n      </dataset_metadata>\n\n      <image_metadata>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n        - filename (text)\n        - width (integer)\n        - height (integer)\n        - thumbnail_path (text)\n        - total_gt_boxes (integer)\n        - total_pred_boxes (integer)\n        - has_fp (boolean)\n        - has_fn (boolean)\n        - is_perfect (boolean)\n      </image_metadata>\n\n      <classes>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n        - name (text, unique per dataset)\n        - total_gt_count (integer)\n        - total_pred_count (integer)\n        - tp_count (integer)\n        - fp_count (integer)\n        - fn_count (integer)\n        - recall (float)\n        - precision (float)\n        - fpr (float)\n        - f1_score (float)\n      </classes>\n\n      <bounding_boxes>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - image_id (foreign key to image_metadata.id)\n        - class_id (foreign key to classes.id)\n        - type (text, enum: 'ground_truth', 'prediction')\n        - x1 (float)\n        - y1 (float)\n        - x2 (float)\n        - y2 (float)\n        - confidence (float, null for ground truth)\n        - iou (float, null for unmatched boxes)\n        - classification (text, enum: 'tp', 'fp', 'fn', null for unclassified)\n      </bounding_boxes>\n    </tables>\n    <notes>\n      - Database is SQLite (file-based) for simplicity, stored as dataset_analysis.db\n      - Data is cleared/overwritten when a new dataset is loaded\n      - Indexes on filename, class name, and image_id for faster queries\n    </notes>\n  </database_schema>\n\n  <api_endpoints_summary>\n    <dataset>\n      - POST /api/dataset/load: Load and parse dataset from provided path\n        - Request body: { \"dataset_path\": \"/path/to/dataset\", \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"dataset_id\": 1, \"total_images\": 100, \"total_classes\": 5, \"errors\": [] }\n      - GET /api/dataset/metadata/:id: Get dataset metadata and summary statistics\n      - DELETE /api/dataset/:id: Clear dataset from memory/database\n    </dataset>\n\n    <statistics>\n      - GET /api/statistics/:dataset_id: Get all per-class statistics\n        - Response: { \"classes\": [...], \"overall_metrics\": {...}, \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n      - POST /api/statistics/recalculate: Recalculate metrics with new thresholds\n        - Request body: { \"dataset_id\": 1, \"iou_threshold\": 0.6, \"confidence_threshold\": 0.7 }\n        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"statistics\": {...} }\n      - GET /api/statistics/export/:dataset_id: Export statistics to CSV/JSON\n        - Query params: { \"format\": \"csv\" }\n    </statistics>\n\n    <images>\n      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id: Get list of all images with metadata (filename, thumbnail path, basic stats)\n        - Query params: { \"page\": 1, \"per_page\": 20, \"class_filter\": \"car\", \"status_filter\": \"fp\" }\n      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id/:image_id: Get detailed data for single image\n        - Response: { \n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\", \n            \"dimensions\": { \"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080 },\n            \"ground_truth_boxes\": [...],\n            \"prediction_boxes\": [...],\n            \"per_class_stats\": {...},\n            \"thumbnail_path\": \"/static/thumbnails/img_001.jpg\"\n          }\n      - GET /api/images/thumbnail/:dataset_id/:filename: Serve generated thumbnail image\n    </images>\n\n    <classes>\n      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id: Get list of all classes in dataset with counts\n      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id/:class_id: Get detailed statistics for single class\n    </classes>\n\n    <health>\n      - GET /api/health: Check server status and version\n        - Response: { \"status\": \"healthy\", \"version\": \"1.0.0\", \"python_version\": \"3.9.7\", \"flask_version\": \"2.2.3\" }\n    </health>\n  </api_endpoints_summary>\n\n  <ui_layout>\n    <main_structure>\n      - Fixed top navigation bar (100% width, 60px height) with project title and page links\n      - Main content area (fills remaining viewport height) with:\n        - Statistics Dashboard: Single column layout with summary cards, charts, and tables\n        - Image Comparison View: Two-column layout (25% left panel, 75% right panel)\n      - Responsive breakpoints:\n        - Mobile (<768px): Image Comparison View switches to single column (list at top, image view below)\n        - Tablet (768px-1024px): Left panel 30%, right panel 70%\n        - Desktop (>1024px): Left panel 25%, right panel 75%\n      - Footer (optional) with version info and basic help link (fixed at bottom)\n    </main_structure>\n\n    <statistics_dashboard_layout>\n      - Header section with:\n        - Page title (\"Detection Statistics Dashboard\")\n        - Control panel (IoU slider, confidence slider, class filter dropdown)\n        - Export button (CSV/JSON)\n      - Summary cards section (3-4 cards in row):\n        - Total Images Processed\n        - Number of Classes\n        - Average Recall (all classes)\n        - Average Precision (all classes)\n      - Charts section (2 charts per row):\n        - Per-Class Recall (bar chart)\n        - Per-Class Precision (bar chart)\n        - Per-Class FPR (bar chart)\n        - Class Distribution (ground truth vs predictions) (pie/donut chart)\n      - Detailed metrics table section:\n        - Sortable table with columns: Class, GT Count, Pred Count, TP, FP, FN, Recall, Precision, FPR, F1 Score\n        - Pagination for tables with >20 classes\n        - Search box for filtering table rows\n    </statistics_dashboard_layout>\n\n    <image_comparison_layout>\n      <left_panel_layout>\n        - Search bar (top of panel, 100% width)\n        - Filter section (class filter dropdown, status filter radio buttons)\n        - Image list container (scrollable, 100% remaining height):\n          - Each list item: Thumbnail (left) + Filename (right) + status indicator (small color dot: green=perfect, yellow=FP/FN, red=many errors)\n          - Hover state: Highlight background\n          - Active state: Bold text + colored border\n      </left_panel_layout>\n      <right_panel_layout>\n        - Image header section:\n          - Filename and navigation buttons (previous/next)\n          - Toggle buttons (show/hide GT boxes, show/hide prediction boxes, show/hide labels)\n          - Zoom controls (+/-/reset)\n        - Image display container (max height: 70% of viewport):\n          - Dataset Load Modal: Input field for dataset path, load button, validation messages\n      - Error Modal: Displays detailed error information with close button\n      - Help Modal: Basic instructions for using the tool (keyboard shortcuts, IoU explanation)\n      - Export Modal: Options for export format (CSV/JSON) and data scope (all classes/specific classes)\n      - Loading Overlay: Semi-transparent overlay with spinner during data processing\n    </modals_overlays>\n  </ui_layout>\n\n  <design_system>\n    <color_palette>\n      -         window.location.hash = '#dashboard';\n                await this.loadStatistics();\n            } else {\n                this.showError('Failed to load dataset');\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            this.showError('Error loading dataset: ' + error.message);\n        } finally {\n            this.hideLoading();\n        }\n    }\n\n    async loadStatistics() {\n        try {\n            const response = await fetch(`/api/statistics/${this.datasetId}`);\n            const data = await response.json();\n            this.updateDashboard(data);\n            // Update dashboard display state\n            this.showDashboard();\n        } catch (error) {\n            this.showError('Error loading statistics');\n        }\n    }\n\n    updateDashboard(data) {\n        // Update summary cards\n        if (data.overall_metrics) {\n            document.getElementById('summary-total-images').textContent = data.overall_metrics.total_gt_boxes || '-';\n            document.getElementById('summary-total-classes').textContent = data.classes?.length || '-';\n            document.getElementById('summary-avg-recall').textContent = data.overall_metrics.recall !== undefined ? data.overall_metrics.recall.toFixed(3) : '-';\n            document.getElementById('summary-avg-precision').textContent = data.overall_metrics.precision !== undefined ? data.overall_metrics.precision.toFixed(3) : '-';\n        }\n\n        // Update metrics table\n        this.updateMetricsTable(data.classes || []);\n\n        // Update charts\n        this.updateCharts(data.classes || []);\n    }\n\n    updateMetricsTable(classes) {\n        const tableBody = document.getElementById('metrics-table-body');\n        if (!tableBody) return;\n\n        if (classes.length === 0) {\n            tableBody.innerHTML = '<tr><td colspan=\"10\" class=\"text-center text-muted\">No data available.</td></tr>';\n            return;\n        }\n\n        tableBody.innerHTML = classes.map(cls => `\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>${this.escapeHtml(cls.name)}</strong></td>\n                <td>${cls.total_gt_count || 0}</td>\n                <td>${cls.total_pred_count || 0}</td>\n                <td>${cls.tp_count || 0}</td>\n                <td>${cls.fp_count || 0}</td>\n                <td>${cls.fn_count || 0}</td>\n                <td>${cls.recall !== undefined ? cls.recall.toFixed(3) : '-'}</td>\n                <td>${cls.precision !== undefined ? cls.precision.toFixed(3) : '-'}</td>\n                <td>${cls.fpr !== undefined ? cls.fpr.toFixed(3) : '-'}</td>\n                <td>${cls.f1_score !== undefined ? cls.f1_score.toFixed(3) : '-'}</td>\n            </tr>\n        `).join('');\n    }\n\n    updateCharts(classes) {\n        if (classes.length === 0) return;\n\n        const labels = classes.map(cls => cls.name);\n        const recallData = classes.map(cls => cls.recall !== undefined ? cls.recall : 0);\n        const precisionData = classes.map(cls => cls.precision !== undefined ? cls.precision : 0);\n        const fprData = classes.map(cls => cls.fpr !== undefined ? cls.fpr : 0);\n\n        // Generate colors for each class\n        const colors = this.generateColors(classes.length);\n\n        // Update recall chart\n        this.createOrUpdateChart('recall-chart', 'Recall', labels, recallData, colors);\n\n        // Update precision chart\n        this.createOrUpdateChart('precision-chart', 'Precision', labels, precisionData, colors);\n\n        // Update FPR chart\n        this.createOrUpdateChart('fpr-chart', 'False Positive Rate', labels, fprData, colors);\n    }\n\n    createOrUpdateChart(canvasId, label, labels, data, colors) {\n        const canvas = document.getElementById(canvasId);\n        if (!canvas) return;\n\n        // Set canvas height explicitly for Chart.js to render properly\n        canvas.style.height = '300px';\n        canvas.style.width = '100%';\n\n        const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n        // Check if chart already exists and destroy it\n        if (canvas.chart) {\n            canvas.chart.destroy();\n        }\n\n        // Create new chart with enhanced tooltip configuration\n        canvas.chart = new Chart(ctx, {\n            type: 'bar',\n            data: {\n                labels: labels,\n                datasets: [{\n                    label: label,\n                    data: data,\n                    backgroundColor: colors.map(c => c.background),\n                    borderColor: colors.map(c => c.border),\n                    borderWidth: 2\n                }]\n            },\n            options: {\n                responsive: true,\n                maintainAspectRatio: false,\n                scales: {\n                    y: {\n                        beginAtZero: true,\n                        max: 1,\n                        grid: {\n                            color: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)'\n                        }\n                    },\n                    x: {\n                        grid: {\n                            display: false\n                        }\n                    }\n                },\n                plugins: {\n                    legend: {\n                        display: false\n                    },\n                    tooltip: {\n                        enabled: true,\n                        backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)',\n                        titleColor: '#fff',\n                        bodyColor: '#fff',\n                        borderColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2)',\n                        borderWidth: 1,\n                        padding: 12,\n                        cornerRadius: 6,\n                        callbacks: {\n                            label: function(context) {\n                                return `${context.dataset.label}: ${context.parsed.y.toFixed(3)}`;\n                            },\n                            title: function(context) {\n                                return context[0].label;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    generateColors(count) {\n        const colors = [];\n        const baseHues = [210, 150, 45, 0, 270, 180, 300, 330, 30, 60]; // Blue, green, orange, red, purple, cyan, magenta, pink, brown, yellow\n\n        for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n            const hue = baseHues[i % baseHues.length];\n            const saturation = 70;\n            const lightness = 50;\n            colors.push({\n                background: `hsla(${hue}, ${saturation}%, ${lightness}%, 0.7)`,\n                border: `hsla(${hue}, ${saturation}%, ${lightness - 10}%, 1)`\n            });\n        }\n\n        return colors;\n    }\n\n    escapeHtml(text) {\n        const div = document.createElement('div');\n        div.textContent = text;\n        return div.innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    showLoading() {\n        const overlay = document.getElementById('loading-overlay') || this.createLoadingOverlay();\n        overlay.classList.remove('d-none');\n    }\n\n    hideLoading() {\n        const overlay = document.getElementById('loading-overlay');\n        if (overlay) {\n            overlay.classList.add('d-none');\n        }\n    }\n\n    createLoadingOverlay() {\n        const overlay = document.createElement('div');\n        overlay.id = 'loading-overlay';\n        overlay.className = 'loading-overlay';\n        overlay.innerHTML = '<div class=\"spinner\"></div>';\n        document.body.appendChild(overlay);\n        return overlay;\n    }\n\n    showError(message) {\n        alert(message); // TODO: Implement proper error modal\n    }\n\n    async recalculateStatistics() {\n        if (!this.datasetId) return;\n\n        try {\n            this.showLoading();\n\n            const response = await fetch('/api/statistics/recalculate', {\n                method: 'POST',\n                headers: {\n                    'Content-Type': 'application/json'\n                },\n                body: JSON.stringify({\n                    dataset_id: this.datasetId,\n                    iou_threshold: this.iouThreshold,\n                    confidence_threshold: this.confidenceThreshold\n                })\n            });\n\n            const data = await response.json();\n\n            if (data.success) {\n                await this.loadStatistics();\n            } else {\n                this.showError('Failed to recalculate statistics');\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            this.showError('Error recalculating statistics: ' + error.message);\n        } finally {\n            this.hideLoading();\n        }\n    }\n\n    filterMetricsTable(searchTerm) {\n        const tableBody = document.getElementById('metrics-table-body');\n        if (!tableBody) return;\n\n        const rows = tableBody.querySelectorAll('tr');\n        rows.forEach(row => {\n            const classNameCell = row.querySelector('td:first-child');\n            if (classNameCell) {\n                const className = classNameCell.textContent.toLowerCase();\n                if (className.includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())) {\n                    row.style.display = '';\n                } else {\n                    row.style.display = 'none';\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    async exportStatistics(format) {\n        if (!this.datasetId) {\n            this.showError('No dataset loaded');\n            return;\n        }\n\n        try {\n            const response = await fetch(`/api/statistics/export/${this.datasetId}?format=${format}`);\n\n            if (response.ok) {\n                const blob = await response.blob();\n                const url = window.URL.createObjectURL(blob);\n                const a = document.createElement('a');\n                a.href = url;\n                a.download = `statistics_dataset_${this.datasetId}.${format}`;\n                document.body.appendChild(a);\n                a.click();\n                window.URL.revokeObjectURL(url);\n                document.body.removeChild(a);\n            } else {\n                const errorData = await response.json();\n                this.showError(errorData.error || 'Failed to export statistics');\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            this.showError('Error exporting statistics: ' + error.message);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Initialize application when DOM is ready\ndocument.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {\n    new DetectionAnalyzer();\n});\n","numLines":457,"startLine":1,"totalLines":457}}}
